content.problemStatement:
You are given a directed graph with NNN nodes numbered 111 to NNN, and an array AAA of size NNN.

The graph is such that there is an edge directed from node iii to node (i+j)(i + j)(i+j) for all 1≤j≤Ai1 \le j \le A_i1≤j≤Ai​. No other edges are present in the graph.

Handle QQQ queries of the form:


Given nodes XXX and YYY, find the minimum number of edges on a path from node XXX to node YYY. If it is impossible to go from XXX to YYY, output −1-1−1 instead.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN and QQQ - the number of nodes in the graph, and the number of queries respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ - the array describing the graph's structure.
Each of the next QQQ lines contain 222 integers, XXX and YYY - a query asking for the minimum number of edges in a path from XXX to YYY.




Output Format
For each query, output on a new line, the minimum number of edges in a path from XXX to YYY or −1-1−1 if it is impossible to reach XXX from YYY.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of QQQ over all test cases both do not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 222 : The graph is depicted in the picture :  


Query 111 : We can take the directed path from 111 to 222 and then 222 to 555 for a length of 222. There exists no shorter path, hence the distance between 111 and 555 is 222.


Query 222 : 222 and 444 are connected with a directed edge from 222 to 444, hence we can simply use that for a distance of 111.


Query 444 : There doesn't exist any path from 333 to 444.
